Class Grid

java.lang.Object
simulation.entities.Grid
public class Grid extends Object
Two-dimensional grid of Cell objects that represents the simulation terrain.

The grid supports two topology modes:

  • Bounded — cells at the border have no neighbours beyond the edge.
  • Toroidal — the left edge wraps to the right edge, and the top edge wraps to the bottom edge, creating a doughnut-shaped surface.

The grid can also grow dynamically (bounded mode only, up to MAX_WIDTH × MAX_HEIGHT) when the camera scrolls near an edge.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    DEFAULT_HEIGHT
    Default grid height used when no size is specified.
    static final int
    DEFAULT_WIDTH
    Default grid width used when no size is specified.
    static final int
    MAX_HEIGHT
    Maximum allowed grid height in cells.
    static final int
    MAX_WIDTH
    Maximum allowed grid width in cells.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Grid()
    Creates a bounded grid with DEFAULT_WIDTH × DEFAULT_HEIGHT cells.
    Grid(int width, int height)
    Creates a bounded grid of the given size filled with randomly typed cells.
    Grid(int width, int height, boolean toroidal)
    Creates a grid with the given size, topology, and random cell content.

      public static final int MAX_WIDTH
      Maximum allowed grid width in cells.

      public static final int MAX_HEIGHT
      Maximum allowed grid height in cells.

      Default grid width used when no size is specified.

      public static final int DEFAULT_HEIGHT
      Default grid height used when no size is specified.

  • Constructor Details

    • Grid

      public Grid(int width, int height)
      Creates a bounded grid of the given size filled with randomly typed cells.
      Parameters:
      width - number of columns (> 0)
      height - number of rows (> 0)

    • Grid

      public Grid(int width, int height, boolean toroidal)
      Creates a grid with the given size, topology, and random cell content.
      Parameters:
      width - number of columns
      height - number of rows
      toroidal - true for toroidal (wrap-around) topology

    • Grid

      public Grid()
      Creates a bounded grid with DEFAULT_WIDTH × DEFAULT_HEIGHT cells.

  • Method Details

    • getCell

      public Cell getCell(int x, int y)
      Returns the cell at (x, y).

      In toroidal mode coordinates wrap around automatically. In bounded mode returns null if out of bounds.

      Parameters:
      x - column
      y - row
      Returns:
      cell, or null if out of bounds in bounded mode

    • setCell

      public void setCell(int x, int y, Cell c)
      Replaces the cell at (x, y). Does nothing if out of bounds.
      Parameters:
      x - column
      y - row
      c - new cell

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid(int x, int y)
      Returns true if (x, y) is within the current grid bounds. Always true in toroidal mode after wrapping.
      Parameters:
      x - column
      y - row
      Returns:
      validity flag

    • expand

      public void expand(int left, int top, int right, int bottom)
      Expands the grid by adding new rows/columns on the requested sides. Has no effect in toroidal mode (the grid wraps and never needs to grow). Growth is capped so the grid never exceeds MAX_WIDTH × MAX_HEIGHT.
      Parameters:
      left - columns to add on the left
      top - rows to add at the top
      right - columns to add on the right
      bottom - rows to add at the bottom

    • getWidth

      public int getWidth()
      Returns:
      number of columns

    • getHeight

      public int getHeight()
      Returns:
      number of rows

    • isToroidal

      public boolean isToroidal()
      Returns true if this grid uses toroidal (wrap-around) topology.
      Returns:
      toroidal flag

    • setToroidal

      public void setToroidal(boolean toroidal)
      Switches topology mode. Changing to toroidal on an already-populated grid preserves all existing cell data.
      Parameters:
      toroidal - true for toroidal topology
